title: CausalSeed
description: Cross-era causal seed. A seed is a small narrative payload (a sentence, an object, a promise) planted in one scene that may echo into other scenes decades later. The Resolver is the only component that decides when a seed's trigger_condition matches and activates it. Active seeds are listed in the world snapshot; fired seeds are retired.

source_event.description: ResolverOutcomeId of the event that created the seed.

description.description: Plain prose description of the seed (the thing being planted).

type.description: Category of trigger. 'composite' means the predicates are AND-joined.

predicate.description: Predicate string the Resolver evaluates. DSL subset: subject/operator/value.

minEcho.description: Minimum echo_intensity required for the seed to fire.

target_scenes.description: SceneIds this seed is allowed to echo into. Resolver will not fire the seed outside this list.

echo_intensity.description: Current intensity of the echo. Decays with time, surges when reinforced. Clamped to [0,1].

is_secret.description: True iff the player should not be told the seed exists until it fires. Used to hide plot twists.

firedAt.description: Event sequence at which the seed fired; null while dormant.

firedInSceneId.description: SceneId in which the seed fired; null while dormant.

eraSpan.description: Optional era constraints; the seed only fires when the active era is within [from, to] inclusive.

linkedCharacterIds.description: Characters implicated in the seed; used for memory recall weighting.

decayRate.description: Per-turn echo_intensity multiplier. 0 = eternal, 1 = instant decay. Default 0.02.

tags.description: Free-form tags for memory-recall filtering. Linter warns if any tag is not in the project glossary.

allOf.description: If firedAt is set, firedInSceneId MUST also be set (and vice versa).
If is_secret is true, the seed MUST NOT appear in any client-visible response until it has fired.
